title: Research Paper
description: Data contract for the research-paper format: a self-contained, themeable, interactive HTML rendering of a formal academic / journal article. The whole #report-data instance is rendered by an inline render() into a centred serif 'paper page'. Apparatus is academic: title block with superscript affiliations, abstract box + keywords, numbered sections/subsections, inline numbered citations, numbered HTML equations, interactive figures (charts), ruled tables, and a numbered References list. Inline citations live inside para.html as the marker [[cite:<refId>]] (and grouped [[cite:r1,r3]]) which the renderer rewrites into .rp-cite links to references[].id.

aipaper.description: Platform metadata required by the AlexandrAI server. Identifies the template/skill versions that produced the document and classifies it against the publishing taxonomy (language + category + topics).

templateVersion.description: Template contract version; must be exactly 'research-paper@1' for this shell.

skillVersion.description: Version string of the skill that generated the document, e.g. 'alexandrai-research-publishing@0.1.0'.

researchAudit.description: Deep Research Gate audit trail. evidenceStatus defaults to sufficient. Use scarce only when a documented exhaustion audit justifies an evidence-limited paper; the validator still requires every cited reference to be fully read.

profile.description: Research depth profile. Use 'deep' for normal autonomous publishing and 'exhaustive' for broader sweeps.

evidenceStatus.description: Evidence sufficiency route. Omit or set 'sufficient' for the normal Deep Research Gate. Set 'scarce' only after exhaustive searching fails and an exhaustion audit is supplied.

studyMode.description: Study mode actually used, such as empirical_measurement, reproducible_benchmark, literature_review, taxonomy, conceptual_synthesis, position_paper, or research_agenda.

searchDate.description: Date the research sweep was performed, ISO 8601 preferred.

searches.description: Search log entries. Include AlexandrAI graph searches and external scholarly/web/official searches.

screenedSources.description: Sources screened from titles, abstracts, summaries, or result snippets.

fullReadSources.description: Sources read deeply enough to judge relevance and quality. Every final reference must appear here by id.

citationChasing.description: Backward and forward citation chasing records from key sources.

contradictoryEvidence.description: Contradictory, limiting, or negative evidence deliberately checked.

claimLedger.description: Major claims mapped to full-read source ids, reasoning: ids, or computation: ids.

exhaustion.description: Required when evidenceStatus is scarce. Records why the paper is evidence-limited instead of padding sources.

meta.description: Shared report-chrome object common to every format in the system. Brand-neutral: keep {{ORG}} / {{PROJECT}} / {{AUTHOR}} placeholders unless real values are supplied. meta.theme sets the initial brand accent applied to <html data-theme> on render.

reportType.description: Document-type label; for this format use 'Research Paper'.

title.description: Document title for the <title> tag / chrome (usually mirrors paper.title).

subtitle.description: Short standfirst / venue line for chrome.

theme.description: Initial brand accent preset. Default is "black" — only pick a colour when the user explicitly asks for one.

paper.description: The article front-matter: title, author list with affiliation links, the affiliation directory, abstract, keywords, and DOI-like identifiers.

title.description: Full paper title (rendered large in serif in .rp-title).

authors.description: Ordered author list. Each author's affiliation markers are rendered as superscripts keyed to affiliations[].id.

affil.description: Affiliation ids this author belongs to (must match affiliations[].id); rendered as superscript markers in order.

corresponding.description: If true, marked as the corresponding author (asterisk + corresponding-author line).

email.description: Contact email shown on the corresponding-author line (optional).

affiliations.description: Affiliation directory rendered as a numbered/lettered list under the author line; ids are referenced by authors[].affil.

id.description: Stable affiliation id used as the superscript marker (e.g. 'a', 'b' or '1', '2').

date.description: Submission / version date shown in the article masthead (optional; falls back to meta.date).

articleId.description: Optional external identifier shown in mono in the masthead. Use only a REAL id (such as a DOI); otherwise omit it. Never invent an arXiv number — the AlexandrAI site assigns the canonical id on upload.

abstract.description: Abstract paragraph(s); plain prose rendered in the bordered/tinted .rp-abstract box. Inline HTML emphasis (<em>/<strong>) allowed; no citations in the abstract.

keywords.description: English-only ASCII keyword terms rendered on the 'Keywords:' line under the abstract. Translate non-English concepts before upload.

sections.description: Ordered, recursively-nestable numbered sections. Each section renders a serif heading prefixed by its number and a stack of content blocks; child sections become subsections (e.g. 1.1, 1.2).

references.description: Numbered reference list (the citation targets). Render order defines [n]; each entry's id is the target of [[cite:id]] markers and of citation click-to-scroll.

id.description: Stable reference id referenced by inline [[cite:id]] markers.

authors.description: Author string for the reference, e.g. 'Okabe T., Lindgren A.'.

url.description: Optional link; rendered as an external-link affordance (kept brand-neutral / may be a DOI-style string).

source.description: Search surface, e.g. alexandrai, scholarly, web, official, dataset.

query.description: Exact query or search angle used.

resultCount.description: Number of results observed or retained.

purpose.description: Why this search angle was run.

id.description: Stable source id used in the audit and references.

type.description: Source type, e.g. peer-reviewed, technical-report, official-documentation, dataset-or-benchmark, alexandrai-paper.

decision.description: Screening decision, e.g. include, exclude, maybe, screened.

rationale.description: Why the source was included, excluded, or retained for deeper reading.

url.description: Optional URL, DOI, or stable identifier.

id.description: Stable source id; final references must match one of these ids.

keyClaims.description: Claims, data, or methods extracted from the full read.

limitations.description: Method or relevance limitations found during reading.

usedInPaper.description: Whether the source is used in the final paper.

url.description: Optional URL, DOI, or stable identifier.

seedId.description: Source id used as the citation-chasing seed.

direction.description: Backward follows references; forward finds later work that cites the seed.

linkedSourceId.description: Source discovered or checked through citation chasing.

decision.description: How the linked source was handled.

rationale.description: Why the linked source mattered or was discarded.

sourceId.description: Source id for contradictory, limiting, or negative evidence.

implication.description: How this changed the paper's scope, caveats, or interpretation.

expandedQueries.description: Expanded query variants attempted after initial results were scarce.

searchedSources.description: Search surfaces checked, such as AlexandrAI, Crossref, arXiv, official documentation, standards bodies, or dataset registries.

emptyOrLowYieldQueries.description: Queries that produced no usable source or only weakly related results.

whyMoreSourcesWereNotAvailable.description: Concrete explanation for why the available source base is genuinely scarce.

scopeAdjustment.description: How the paper was narrowed or reframed into an evidence-limited contribution.

claim.description: Major factual, quantitative, comparative, or methodological claim.

kind.description: Claim type. Factual claims require at least one full-read source id; reasoning: supports are only valid for inference claims with a reasoning note.

supports.description: Full-read source ids, reasoning: ids, or computation: ids that support the claim. Do not use reasoning: for ordinary factual claims.

location.description: Paper section where the claim appears.

reasoning.description: Required when kind is inference and supports include a reasoning: id.

computation.description: Optional description of a reproducible computation when supports include a computation: id.

section.description: A numbered section or subsection. 'number' is the display label (e.g. '2', '2.1'); 'children' (if present) render as subsections.

id.description: Stable section id; used as the scroll anchor and the outline-nav target.

blocks.description: Ordered content blocks for this section.

block.description: One content block. Discriminated by 'type'. para = justified serif paragraph (HTML, may carry [[cite:id]] citation markers); equation = numbered centred HTML/Unicode equation; figure = an interactive chart (or an embedded image) with caption; table = a ruled booktabs-style table; list = an ordered/unordered list.

html.description: para: paragraph HTML (inline <em>/<strong>/<code> allowed). Citation markers [[cite:refId]] or grouped [[cite:r1,r3]] are rewritten by the renderer into .rp-cite links. equation: the equation body as HTML using <sup>/<sub>/Unicode math (NO external math library).

number.description: equation: display number rendered right-aligned as '(n)'.

id.description: figure/table: stable id; used as the scroll anchor and referenced by captions.

caption.description: figure/table: caption text rendered after the bold 'Figure N.' / 'Table N.' label.

chart.description: figure: the interactive chart definition. A figure carries EITHER a chart OR an image.

image.description: figure: embed an image instead of a chart (photo, micrograph, diagram, screenshot). Prefer a data: URI so the document stays self-contained; external http(s) URLs may be blocked.

src.description: Image source — preferably a data: URI (e.g. 'data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgo...'). An http(s) URL works only if the host permits it.

alt.description: Accessible alternative text (falls back to the caption).

columns.description: table: column definitions (header label + alignment + optional numeric flag for sorting).

numeric.description: If true, the column sorts numerically and uses tabular figures.

rows.description: table: row objects keyed by columns[].key.

ordered.description: list: render as an ordered <ol> when true, else an unordered list.

items.description: list: item strings (inline HTML + [[cite:id]] markers allowed).

chart.description: An interactive figure chart drawn with inline SVG (no chart library). Every chart supports hover tooltips with exact values; multi-series kinds add a clickable legend that toggles series. 'control' optionally adds a per-figure parameter control. The data shape depends on 'kind' — see the per-property notes below. Renderer dispatch lives in drawFigure(); axis-less kinds (pie/donut/radar/heatmap) skip x/y axes.

kind.description: Chart type, governing the data shape consumed. POINT kinds (series[].points): line/scatter/area/stacked-area/step use [x,y]; bubble uses [x,y,r]. CATEGORY kinds (categories[] + series[].values[]): bar (grouped), stacked-bar (stacked), horizontal-bar (rotated bar), waterfall (signed deltas). SLICE kinds (single series.slices[]): pie, donut. radar uses axes[] + series[].values[]. histogram uses bins[]. box uses categories[] + series[].boxes[]. heatmap uses xLabels[]/yLabels[]/z[][].

xLabel.description: X-axis title (ignored by axis-less kinds: pie/donut/radar/heatmap).

categories.description: bar/stacked-bar/horizontal-bar/box/waterfall: category labels along the category axis (aligned to each series' values[]/boxes[]).

axes.description: radar: spoke labels (one per axis), aligned to each series' values[].

xLabels.description: heatmap: column labels along the x-axis (length = number of columns in z).

yLabels.description: heatmap: row labels along the y-axis (length = number of rows in z).

z.description: heatmap: matrix of numeric cell values; z[row][col] aligned to yLabels[row]/xLabels[col]. Cell tint interpolates the brand accent across the value range.

bins.description: histogram: precomputed bins. Each bin is the half-open interval [x0, x1) with the given count.

count.description: Number of observations in the bin.

total.description: waterfall: when true, append a final 'total' bar equal to the running cumulative sum of the deltas.

series.description: Plotted data series; each gets a legend entry and a distinct accent (the brand accent for the primary series). Toggling a legend entry hides/shows that series. Pie/donut use a single series carrying slices[]; histogram and heatmap ignore series.

color.description: Semantic accent token for the series (default cycles starting at the brand accent 'navy'). No 4th invented colour.

points.description: line/scatter/area/stacked-area/step: ordered [x, y] numeric pairs. bubble: [x, y, r] triples (r = bubble radius value).

values.description: bar/stacked-bar/horizontal-bar/waterfall: numeric values aligned to chart.categories. radar: numeric values aligned to chart.axes. waterfall values are signed deltas.

slices.description: pie/donut: the wedges of the single series. Each slice is a named value; optional per-slice color token (else cycles the semantic palette).

value.description: Slice magnitude (non-negative); rendered proportional to the sum.

color.description: Optional semantic accent token for this slice.

boxes.description: box: one five-number summary per category (aligned to chart.categories).

outliers.description: Optional individual outlier points beyond the whiskers.

control.description: Optional per-figure parameter control. kind:'slider' overlays a fitted model curve y = f(x; param) recomputed live as the slider moves (point kinds: line/scatter/area/step); kind:'datatable' adds a show/hide toggle that reveals the figure's underlying values as a small ruled table (supported wherever a tabular view makes sense).

model.description: slider: overlay model — 'linear' draws y = value·x; 'power' draws y = c·x^value fitted to the first series. Recomputed live.

unit.description: slider: unit suffix for the live read-out.

meta.title: Tail-Latency Behaviour of Sharded Ledger Commit Under Bursty Load
meta.subtitle: A measurement study of distributed banking-core transaction pipelines
meta.author: {{AUTHOR}}

paper.title: Tail-Latency Behaviour of Sharded Ledger Commit Under Bursty Load: A Measurement Study

affiliations.name: Institute for Distributed Systems, Northgate University
Centre for Financial Computing, Meridian Institute of Technology

paper.abstract: We present a measurement study of commit latency in a horizontally sharded ledger across 18 weeks of production-shadow traffic. Under bursty arrivals the 99.9th-percentile commit time degrades up to 7x faster than the median, driven primarily by cross-shard coordination rather than disk I/O. We characterise the degradation as a power law in offered load and show that a bounded-staleness read path removes 63% of the tail without weakening settlement guarantees.

blocks.html: Latency tails dominate the user-visible cost of distributed commit paths. Prior work establishes that medians are poor predictors of tail behaviour under contention [[cite:dean2013]], yet most ledger benchmarks still report means [[cite:nishi2024,brandt2025]].

items: a longitudinal characterisation of p50/p99/p99.9 commit latency under real bursts;
a fitted power-law model relating tail latency to offered load;
a bounded-staleness read path that cuts the tail without weakening guarantees.

blocks.html: We model offered load L against the 99.9th-percentile commit time Tp99.9 and fit a power law, following the contention analysis of [[cite:dean2013]].

blocks.caption: Commit latency percentiles versus offered load (transactions per second). Tail percentiles rise super-linearly; drag the exponent to overlay the fitted power-law model.

blocks.html: Table 1 summarises the per-region degradation. Cross-shard coordination accounts for the majority of the tail in every region [[cite:nishi2024]].

blocks.caption: Tail-latency degradation factor (p99.9 / p50) by region at 16 ktps.
Where the tail goes: mean contribution to p99.9 commit time by stage. Toggle the legend to isolate a stage.

blocks.html: A bounded-staleness read path removes 63% of the p99.9 tail while preserving settlement correctness [[cite:brandt2025]]. Future work will extend the model to multi-region quorum reconfiguration.

references.title: Coordination cost in sharded financial ledgers
